sprint: Scene Viewport — the Page Itself Honours a Scene's Aspect Ratio
task: Direct follow-up to Multiple Artwork Places Per Page: 'we need to honour the commitments of builder' -- the whole page shape must genuinely become landscape (wider than tall), not just an inner rect on a still-portrait page. renderer/slideRenderer.js's fixed 1080x1350 module-level canvas is now resolved per-Slide via a new Scene Viewport concept (SCENE_VIEWPORT_BY_ASPECT, mirroring World Builder v2's real ASPECT_RATIOS table) -- deliberately named around a general 'Scene Viewport' idea rather than 'canvas size'/'aspect ratio' specifically, per explicit user direction, so this stays compatible with a future generalized presentation-surface investigation. getCanvasSize(s) keeps its exact public name/signature; Cover/Hook/End Scene-blueprint pages are explicitly gated to always stay portrait regardless of the active theme's Layouts. A new per-canvas-element opt-in flag (cv.__vihuAdaptiveViewport) means only the live editor canvas and thumbnail generation ever resize -- Publish/Export's own three files needed zero code changes, confirmed by tracing every call site. Two real bugs were found and fixed during implementation, not assumed away: js/thumbnails.js's manual presizing would have been silently clobbered by init()'s own unconditional reset, fixed by opting its temp canvas into the same adaptive mechanism the live canvas uses; and the existing multi_place_test.js regression script's own pixel sampler hardcoded a 1080/1350 DPR-scale divisor that broke once a landscape page's logical size became genuinely 1350x1080, fixed to read the real resolved size dynamically. A LAYOUT_RECT['full-bleed'] discrepancy (authored for a portrait canvas though its real aspect is 1080x1920) was also corrected while touching this table -- dormant, no live theme reaches it. Scope deliberately bounded to the editor/page canvas only; Publish/Export keep their own fixed destination formats unchanged. Verified via a new Playwright test building a real Landscape Dual Holder Scene through the real ProjectCompiler, confirming the live canvas genuinely resizes to 1350x1080 with the correct inline aspect-ratio, both Places render non-overlapping, a Cover-role page stays fixed portrait, and reverting to a portrait Scene reverts the canvas. Full regression across the entire existing Creator/Builder/Card-Platform Playwright suite (24 scripts) and both goldenBuild.js suites passes unchanged, zero console errors. See CLAUDE.md's matching entry for the full account.
